_doc: Hand-built faithful-replay fixtures for a UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities HandleAppRequest NullReferenceException. Signatures and stack frames are source-grounded (UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.HandleAppRequest.EndExecute). Substring dispatch, first match wins — specific rules precede generic ones.

rules._doc: Error-level log row repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
The Error-level set answers any logs variant — the exception row is the only diagnostic content.
Pending -> Running -> Faulted transition set; confirms a clean single execution, no diagnostic signal beyond jobs get.

Three spans: RobotJob (NullReferenceException stack with the HandleAppRequest.EndExecute faulting frame), the Handle Apps Request activity span (FullTypeName UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.HandleAppRequest, WorkflowFile=ProcessRequest.xaml, ConnectionMode=RobotJS — proves this is an App-invoked workflow), and the faulting Assign span (Value io_Records.Rows.Count, the null dereference inside the invoked workflow). The WorkflowFile + Assign expression are the cause-disambiguation evidence (in-workflow null vs WorkflowEvents-package bug vs SignalR channel).
Alternate trace-retrieval form (platform traces command); same span payload.
Positional TraceId (32-char hex from the job record). Same span payload.
Substring dispatch routes --state Faulted variants here too; the failing job is the most recent row.
Fallback for flag-order variations. The folder of interest is Shared; the recorded folder-scoped job list is the correct answer for any jobs-list variant.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs and canned responses go stale. Cached per-query within a run.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any uip command not matched by rules returns an empty array. Lets the agent explore beyond the recorded path without aborting.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.
Agent must fetch the failing job to read the System.NullReferenceException surfaced through UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.HandleAppRequest.EndExecute.

Cause disambiguation: the Handle Apps Request span carries WorkflowFile=ProcessRequest.xaml and the faulting Assign span carries Value=io_Records.Rows.Count, together pinning the null dereference to the App-invoked workflow rather than the WorkflowEvents package or the SignalR channel.
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — state transitions confirm a clean single execution.
